lessons.title: First Win: Pro Loop Setup
lessons.body:
**Track:** Advanced  •  **Week 1 Day 1**

**Feedback:** Overlay fades early—finish by feel + ears.  (bandwidth ±28 cents)

### Objectives
- Complete the full pro loop: record → feedback → playback → best take → next step.
- Hit the target calmly (no pushing volume).
- Make one small improvement compared to your last best take (or match it cleanly).
- Prioritize clean onsets and stable tone even under low feedback.

### Warm-up (3–5 min)
- Gentle hum 60–90s: soft, steady airflow.
- NG resonance 60s: relaxed throat, forward buzz.
- Tiny glide 30–60s: small range, zero strain.

### Core drills (8–12 min)
- Drill 1: **match_e4_lock** — 4–6 reps. Lock fast, then keep it still.
- Drill 2: **sustain_a4** — 4–6 reps. Micro-correct; don’t chase.

### Transfer (2–4 min)
- Do 2 reps with reduced feedback (fade/off).
- After each rep: listen once, then adjust only one thing.

### Close (60s)
- Playback your best rep (waveform).
- Save Best Take.
- Read the ‘What next’ action and commit to tomorrow.

### Coaching cues (one thing)
- Your job is calm control, not perfection.
- Let the line guide you, but don’t over-correct—small moves only.
- Save best take based on cleanliness + stability, not just score.

### Troubleshooting
- If the room is noisy or confidence is low: go softer, move closer to mic, and focus on steady breath.
- If you keep missing: reset with a hum, then try again at lower volume.
- If you feel strain: stop and do the recovery day version (warmup + gentle sustain).
coachScript: Aim for repeatable control. Treat each rep like a studio take.
If you drift, return gently—don’t snap.
Finish with one transfer rep: less overlay, more ears.

meta.focus: Calibration & wins — build one clear skill, then do a short transfer rep without leaning on the overlay.
